name: BE-Rocket
university: KU Leuven / VUB / ULB / ULiège / UMons
description: Belgium's national student rocketry team, drawing from six universities. Developing Bossart-II for EuRoC; first Belgian student rocket flew in collaboration between VUB and KU Leuven.

name: Brno Mars Rover
description: Rover team debuting at ERC 2025 with Freya, finishing 11th overall, 2nd in Autonomous Drone Navigation, and 2nd in Sample Collection.

name: CTU Space Research
description: First Czech university team dedicated to high-performance rocketry. Built the largest civilian rocket launch pad in the Czech Republic; developing Illustria hybrid rocket and a future nanosatellite. EuRoC participant since 2022.

name: DanSTAR
description: First European student team to launch a bi-liquid rocket. World record 2021: apogee of 6,545 m. Supported by Copenhagen Suborbitals and a pioneer in student bi-liquid propulsion.

name: Aalto University Satellite Program
description: Finland's leading student satellite programme. Built Aalto-1 (2017, Finland's first satellite, 80+ students) and Suomi-100 (aurora research). Aalto-3 targeting 2027 launch with open-source avionics and S-band radio.

name: Air ESIEA
description: One of France's consistent EuRoC teams, participating since the inaugural 2020 edition. Tested novel flight computers in collaboration with TUDSaT at EuRoC 2024.

name: Supaero Space Section (SSS)
description: 60+ member rocketry team at France's top aerospace school. 8 rockets launched since 2010. Developing Aether (H2O2/ABS hybrid with ONERA); twice awarded Best French High-Tech association. EuRoC and CNES PERSEUS partner.

name: Aristotle Space & Aeronautics Team (A.S.A.T.)
description: Founded 2015 — Greece's first student aerospace team with 70+ members. First Greek team to launch high-powered rockets at EuRoC (2021, 2022). Top-10 in Europe in both rocketry and aeronautics competitions.

name: ASPiRE
description: 54-member rocketry team. EuRoC 2025: 14th overall and winner of the Payload Award (1st place). SAC 2021: 3rd in SRAD 10k Solid Motor category.

name: SpaceDot / AcubeSAT
description: 40+ member team developing AcubeSAT, selected for ESA Fly Your Satellite! Cycle 3. The 3U+ CubeSat carries a lab-on-a-chip to study microgravity and radiation effects on cells in orbit.

name: BME Suborbitals
university: Budapest University of Technology and Economics (BME)
description: 80-member team (including PhD students) founded 2021. EuRoC 2024: Prometheus to 3,242 m. EuRoC 2025: Aurora rocket with own solid propellant, active airbrakes, and a CubeSat payload. Winner of International Rocket Cup.

name: EirSpace
description: Ireland's first student rocketry organisation, founded February 2023 with 62 students. EuRoC 2024 debut. Also pursuing the Collegiate Propulsive Lander Challenge.

name: UCD Space — EIRSAT-1
description: Built EIRSAT-1 (launched Dec 2023 on Falcon 9) — Ireland's first satellite, developed under ESA Fly Your Satellite! programme. Completed 4,500+ orbits; gamma-ray burst science mission successfully concluded.

name: CubeSat Team PoliTo
description: One of Europe's longest-running student satellite programmes (since 2008). e-st@r launched on Vega's maiden flight (2012) — first Italian CubeSat. e-st@r-II flew on ESA Fly Your Satellite! Phase 2.

name: PoliTo Rocket Team
description: Regular EuRoC competitor from Italy's second-largest technical university. Flew ICARUS in the DART category at EuRoC 2023 and continues developing advanced propulsion systems.

name: Skyward Experimental Rocketry
description: 170+ member team founded 2012. EuRoC overall winner 2022 and 2024; 2nd overall 2021. 10+ launches. Developing Orion (hybrid) for EuRoC 2025. One of Europe's most successful student rocket teams.

name: DARE — Delft Aerospace Rocket Engineering
description: Founded 2001 — one of the world's most advanced student rocket teams. Stratos II+ (2015): 21.5 km European student altitude record. Currently developing Stratos IV/V targeting cryogenic propulsion. Regular EuRoC competitor.

name: Delfi Space — TU Delft
description: Built Delfi-C3 (2008) — first Dutch satellite, still operational after 15+ years. Da Vinci Satellite (80 members) selected for ESA Fly Your Satellite! 4, launching 2026 with an interactive school education mission.

name: AGH Space Systems
description: One of Europe's most decorated student space organisations. ERC 2023 winner, ERC 2024 runner-up, URC 2024 winner, IRC 2023 winner. Built Poland's first liquid-propellant student rocket ('Turbulence'). Also runs stratospheric balloon missions.

name: PUT Rocketlab
description: 69-member team (founded 2017) with HEXA 4 hybrid rocket competing in Spaceport America Cup's highest category. 1st place in multiple national competitions and FAR-OUT Mojave competitor.

name: PW-Sat
description: Poland's most experienced student satellite team. PW-Sat1 (2012, Vega maiden) — Poland's first satellite. PW-Sat2 (2018): successfully tested deorbit sail. PW-Sat3 in development with proprietary de-orbit drive.

name: Porto Space Team
description: EuRoC 2024 competitor and first Portuguese team to complete a hybrid propulsion system test. Also developing ICARUS, a 1U nanosatellite in collaboration with INEGI under the CubeSat Portugal programme.

name: RED — Rocket Experiment Division (AeroTéc)
description: EuRoC participant since 2021 and Portugal's leading rocketry team. Won EuRoC Payload Award 2023; 2nd overall at EuRoC 2023 with Adamastor (1st in S3 category). First Portuguese team to win an EuRoC prize.

name: Faraday Rocketry UPV
description: 46-member team that won EuRoC's most challenging controlled landing category with Genesis rocket on a minimal budget. Aims to become the first European student team to reach the Karman line.

name: STAR UC3M
description: 80-member Student Team for Aerospace and Rocketry at UC3M. Regular EuRoC competitor with multiple launches and a strong focus on advanced propulsion and recovery systems.

name: AESIR (Association of Engineering Students in Rocketry)
description: First Swedish team at EuRoC (2023). ~60 members developing high-speed rockets with the long-term goal of breaking the European student altitude record. Signý reached 3,384 m at 352 m/s.

name: MIST Satellite
description: CubeSat with 5 scientific and technical experiments, initiated 2015. Targeting a 2027 launch to coincide with KTH's 200th anniversary.

name: ARIS (Akademische Raumfahrtinitiative Schweiz)
description: Multi-campus Swiss team (founded 2017) competing at every EuRoC edition since 2021. Projects: ASTERIA (bi-liquid, 9 km target), NAUTILUS (Unmanned Underwater Vehicle for polar research), SAGE (modular satellite platform).

name: ASCLEPIOS Association
description: Student-run analog astronaut mission organisation, hosting annual 16-day lunar base simulations in a Swiss Alps fortress. 100+ officers globally. Runs ~12 science experiments per mission from external MSc/PhD teams worldwide — free of charge. Asclepios VI planned July 2026.

name: EPFL Rocket Team (ERT)
description: ~80 active members across 5 faculties. EuRoC 2021 overall winner. Projects include Firehorn (cryogenic bi-liquid) and Hyperion (bi-liquid R&D). Consistently among Europe's top student rocket teams.

name: EPFL Xplore
description: ~120 students across 10 fields. ERC 2025 overall winner. ERC 2023: 3rd overall + Excellence Award. ERC 2022: 2nd place. One of Europe's top planetary rover teams.

name: Endeavour Rocketry
description: ~80 students developing multiple rocket projects simultaneously: Darwin (supersonic target), Maxwell (liquid engine), Bayes (thrust vector control hopper). EuRoC participant since 2021.

name: Imperial College London Rocketry (ICLR)
description: ~100 students per year. EuRoC 2024: Nimbus 24 placed 2nd in payload, flight performance, and team effort. First European student team to perform Thrust Vector Control with a liquid rocket engine.

name: Project Sunride
description: Holders of the UK Open Altitude Record (36,274 ft / 11 km, set 2019). First UK team at Spaceport America Cup (2018). Winner of the James Barrowman Award. Long-term goal: first UK student team to reach the Karman line.
